authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. federal authority for franchise disclosure obligations, franchise terminology, and the baseline records that shape franchise award, training, and opening-readiness workflows.
used_for: FDD and Item 11 training evidence requirements

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 436, Franchise Rule, Item 11
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the current federal rule text. Item 11 directly names franchisor assistance, computer systems, and training disclosures, including required attendees, training hours, location, completion timing, and refresher obligations.
used_for: refresher and supplemental training blocker handling

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC compliance guidance explains how FDD items, receipt handling, agreement timing, and training disclosures should be operationalized, making it directly relevant to training prerequisite and escalation rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Receipt, agreement, and disclosed-training prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: Statement of Policy Regarding Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA coordinates state franchise registration administrators, and its guidance is the recognized multistate baseline for FDD filing, state addenda, receipt treatment, and disclosure consistency that can affect training obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: State addenda, disclosure consistency, and jurisdiction-sensitive review

authoritative_sources.title: Education Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IFA is the leading franchise industry association and its education programs identify franchise-specific training, Fran-Guard, franchise leadership, coaching, support, and CFE terminology that distinguish this lane from generic learning administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Franchise education terminology and enablement planning
used_for: franchisor, franchisee, and support-role education framing

authoritative_sources.title: About the CEU and ANSI/IACET Standard for Continuing Education and Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Accreditors for Continuing Education and Training
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IACET maintains the CEU and ANSI/IACET continuing education standard, providing a recognized public baseline for measuring organized training, responsible sponsorship, qualified instruction, and learner records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Learner-record, completion, and credential-readiness evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control standard for awareness and training, account management, authorization, access approval, and auditability that constrains LMS, portal, and role-readiness decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Training control, access approval, and audit-evidence checks

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63B-4, Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Authenticator Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-63B-4 is current federal digital identity guidance for authentication and authenticator management, making it directly relevant to franchise portal enrollment, MFA, credential recovery, and access-readiness boundaries.

workflow_stages.name: Training intake and source-of-obligation validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is tied to the correct franchise brand, unit, cohort, role, jurisdiction, FDD Item 11 baseline, franchise agreement training clauses, state addenda, and tenant curriculum version before assigning or validating training.

workflow_stages.name: Curriculum assignment and prerequisite sequencing
workflow_stages.description: Map the learner to required brand-standard modules, operations-manual chapters, instructor-led sessions, field practicum, assessments, make-up work, refresher obligations, and role-based prerequisite gates.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion, credential, and learner-record evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Validate LMS transcripts, attendance rosters, instructor or field-trainer signoffs, assessment scores, policy acknowledgments, certificates, badges, and exception notes using the tenant systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Access, sandbox, and production-readiness checks
workflow_stages.description: Confirm training environment access, franchise portal enrollment, MFA, role grants, POS or back-office sandbox readiness, and production access prerequisites without granting access directly.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness confirmation and go-live handoff
workflow_stages.description: Decide whether training and enablement prerequisites are complete, blocked, deferred, or ready for handoff to opening or steady-state operations while preserving approval boundaries.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority conflict and specialized-lane escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when legal interpretation, state addenda, curriculum waiver, HR issue, safety-critical certification, identity administration, finance, or final opening approval changes the safe answer.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Franchise Disclosure Document Item 11 training disclosure and revision reference
Franchise agreement training clauses, state addenda, and operations-manual training obligations
Tenant curriculum map, role-based learning path, cohort record, training calendar, and prerequisite sequence
LMS transcript, attendance roster, make-up plan, assessment score, quiz or practical exam evidence, and completion timestamp
Instructor-led session signoff, field-practicum checklist, trainer observation note, and brand certification or badge record
Policy acknowledgment, operations-manual acknowledgment, refresher or recertification schedule, and exception or waiver request
IAM ticket, role grant, MFA enrollment, training sandbox access, franchisee portal provisioning, and helpdesk work item
Training readiness summary, credential blocker escalation note, go-live handoff note, deferred-scope matrix, and traceability log
systems_of_record: Franchise CRM or franchisee master-data system
Document repository or e-signature system for FDD, agreement, addenda, and operations-manual acknowledgments
Virtual classroom or instructor-led training attendance system
Assessment, certification, badging, or credential platform
Identity and access management or SSO platform
Training sandbox, POS, back-office, or franchisee portal provisioning system
Unit opening checklist or project-tracking system
Field trainer, operations support, or coach handoff tracker

allowed: Classify training, enablement, and credential-readiness requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate franchise-specific training artifacts, LMS evidence, attendance, assessments, signoffs, acknowledgments, access prerequisites, and dependency status.
Produce onboarding checklists, enablement readiness summaries, go-live handoff notes, and credential blocker escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route work to legal, learning governance, HR, safety, identity, finance, onboarding, field operations, or opening-approval owners when ownership changes.
escalate: Required Item 11 reference, agreement clause, state addendum, LMS transcript, assessment, signoff, acknowledgment, access approval, or opening handoff evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
The request requires legal interpretation, curriculum waiver approval, HR decision, safety-critical or licensed certification, direct access grant, financial approval, or final opening signoff.
Tenant policy, public source guidance, franchise agreement language, or local requirements materially conflict on training timing, completion criteria, access posture, or go-live readiness.
The public baseline is narrowed by jurisdiction, brand controls, role, multi-unit structure, facility context, or tenant operating rules that are missing at runtime.
refuse: Mark training, credentialing, onboarding, or go-live ready without evidence-backed prerequisite closure.
Treat franchise training as generic employee onboarding without franchise-specific Item 11, brand-standard, operations-manual, LMS, role, and unit-opening records.
Approve legal, HR, safety, financial, access, curriculum governance, or opening exceptions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: Generic employee orientation is accepted as equivalent to franchise training even though FDD Item 11, brand-standard modules, and operations-manual acknowledgments are absent.
Attendance is treated as completion even though assessments, make-up work, field-trainer signoffs, or role-based modules remain incomplete.
LMS completion is treated as authority to open even though access approvals, MFA, sandbox practice, policy acknowledgments, or opening-readiness handoff records are missing.
Production portal or POS permissions are granted or accepted before training prerequisites and approver signoffs are complete.
State addenda, franchise agreement clauses, or legal interpretation questions are resolved by the training lane instead of escalated.
Safety-critical, licensed, food-safety, clinical, or other regulated competency claims are treated as normal training completion instead of routed to the credential owner.

The industry label is treated as cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic learning administration language without FDD Item 11, franchisee cohort, operations manual, brand standard, unit-opening, or multi-unit role artifacts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Franchise training obligations vary by brand, agreement, role, jurisdiction, unit type, safety context, and tenant credential policy.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack records the public FTC, eCFR, NASAA, IFA, IACET, and NIST baseline but requires tenant-supplied curriculum, agreement, role, jurisdiction, access, and credential-owner context before final readiness decisions are made.

notes: The research baseline covers franchise workflow, training evidence, terminology, systems of record, and refusal conditions rather than generic learning administration.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because state, tenant, credential, safety, and role differences are explicit retrieval and escalation dependencies.

The lane stops at training readiness validation and handoff packaging. Legal interpretation, curriculum governance approval, direct access administration, HR action, safety-critical certification, financial approval, and final opening approval remain outside the lane.
